diagrams.title: Max-Heap Tree and Array Representation
diagrams.description: Complete binary tree with heap property showing how elements map to array indices
elements: Tree with 7 nodes: root=50, left=30, right=40, children of 30 are 20,25, children of 40 are 35,10
Parent-child relationships shown with arrows
Level order matches array order
diagrams.purpose: Understanding array representation and heap property in max-heap

diagrams.title: Insert Operation Step-by-Step
diagrams.description: Visualizing insert of element 45 into max-heap [50,30,40,20,25,35,10]
elements: Initial heap as complete tree
Step 1: Add 45 at end (index 7) - tree still complete
Step 2: Compare 45 with parent (25 at index 3), 45>25, swap
Step 3: Compare 45 with parent (40 at index 1), 45>40, swap
Step 4: 45 is now root, heap property satisfied
Final heap: [45,30,50,20,25,35,10] after swaps

diagrams.title: Extract-Max Operation
diagrams.description: Visualizing removal of maximum (50) from max-heap [50,30,40,20,25,35,10]
elements: Initial max-heap
Step 1: Store max=50, replace root with last element (10)
Step 2: Heapify root - compare 10 with children 30 and 40, swap with 40
Step 3: Compare 10 with children 20 and 25, swap with 25
Step 4: 10 is at leaf, heap property restored
Final heap: [40,30,35,20,25,10], sorted portion: [50]

diagrams.title: Heap Sort Visualization
diagrams.description: Complete heap sort process showing build-heap and repeated extraction
elements: Original array as tree
Build heap phase (bottom-up heapify)
Repeated extraction with array growing from right
Sorted portion grows from right to left
Final sorted array shown
